title: Rhynchosia zeyheri
taxonomy: Rhynchosia zeyheri is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Rhynchosia, which consists of approximately 250 species found mainly in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Rhynchosia is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a large number of legume species. Rhynchosia zeyheri is native to South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The plant's classification is further defined by its placement in the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group III (APG III) system.
uses: Rhynchosia zeyheri has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which makes it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In addition, Rhynchosia zeyheri has been used to treat fever and respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to make medicinal teas and infusions, which are believed to have therapeutic benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses and effectiveness of Rhynchosia zeyheri.

side_effects: Rhynchosia zeyheri, also known as the horsefly bean or African horsefly bean, is a plant used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world. While it may have potential health benefits, Rhynchosia zeyheri's side effects are not well-documented, and further research is needed to determine its safety for human use. intro: Rhynchosia zeyheri, also known as Zeyher's milkpea, is a perennial climber of the pea family (Fabaceae) native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchosia zeyheri include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rhynchosia zeyheri is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat conditions such as asthma, bronchitis, and skin infections.
